macro_segments.title: Introduction and the Attached vs. Detached Knife Debate

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks and location on the daf
The Mishnah's teaching on knife validity and the gemara's question about attached objects
Rabbi's position that attached knives are invalid; distinction between always-attached and detached-then-attached
Gemara asking for proof of the distinction between mechubar me'ikara and talush u'levasof chibur
Baraita evidence: wheel-powered knives, attached knives, and rocks/reeds protruding from walls
The contradiction and resolution: always-attached vs. detached-then-attached

macro_segments.title: Koach Gavra: Human Energy Requirement for Shechita

micro_segments.title: The wheel issue: baraita contradiction about whether wheel-powered knives are valid
Resolution: pottery wheel vs. water wheel; human energy requirement
Koach rishon and koach sheni: first power vs. second power in causation
Rav Pappa's example of sluice gates and the distinction between direct and indirect causation
Application of koach rishon/sheni to shechita and other halachot

macro_segments.title: Mechubar l'Karka: Defining Attachment Across Different Areas of Halacha

micro_segments.title: Rav sitting behind Rabbi Chiyya who sat before Rabbi; their positions on attached knives
Rabbi's proof from the verse about Abraham taking a knife; gemara's rejection of the proof
Rava's statement: talush u'levasof chibur considered detached for purposes of avodah zarah
The principle that one cannot forbid another's property through bowing; houses become forbidden
Hechsher zera'im: water making food susceptible; vessel on wall case and the debate
Rav Pappa's resolution: distinction between cave wall (mechubar) and constructed wall (not fully mechubar)
Summary of talush u'levasof chibur treatment across avodah zarah, hechsher zera'im, and shechita
Analysis of why different halachot may treat mechubar differently: karka vs. metaltelin concepts

macro_segments.title: The Knife in the Wall and Pressure Concerns

micro_segments.title: Baraita: knife in wall is valid; moving animal's neck over the knife rather than moving knife
Rav Anan citing Shmuel: knife must be above and animal's neck below to avoid pressure
Contradiction: another baraita says knife can be above or below either way
Rav Zevid's resolution: two different cases—detached knife vs. attached knife have different rules
Rav Pappa's exception: birds (light weight) do not require knife above for valid shechita

macro_segments.title: Digression on Reeds and Splinter Concerns

micro_segments.title: Five restrictions on reed use: shechita, bris milah, cutting meat, picking teeth, drying hands
Contradiction: baraita says you can shecht with reed; Rav Pappa distinguishes hard reed from splinter-prone reed
Meat cutting: contextual rules—Rav Pappa with fish (transparent), Rabbah bar Rav Huna with birds (soft)
Hand-wiping (mekanach): concern is flammable material and witchcraft, not splinters

macro_segments.title: Hakol Shochtim u'L'Olam Shochtim: Universal Slaughter and Perpetual Permission

micro_segments.title: Mishnah summary phrase: all things can be slaughtered and always slaughter is valid
Gemara's new reading: all things require shechita; even birds biblically require shechita
Rava attributes to Rabbi Yishmael; meat of desire permitted only in Eretz Yisrael near Mikdash
Rava's inference: in exile, meat of desire should be forbidden again; l'olam shochtim means always permitted
Rav Yosef's objection: meat eating is not the chiddush; distance from Temple justifies eating meat regardless

topical_tags.term: Koach Rishon vs. Koach Sheni (first vs. second power)
Sluice gate example (opening gates and water causation)
Knife position: above vs. below animal's neck
Hakol Shochtim (all things can be slaughtered)
